dhp306:1: He goes to hell, the one who asserts what didn’t take place,
dhp306:2:
as does the one who, having done, says,
‘I didn’t.’
dhp306:4: after death, in the world beyond.
dhp307:1: An ochre robe tied ’round their necks,
dhp307:3: rearise, because of their evil acts,
dhp308:1: Better to eat an iron ball
dhp308:4: unrestrained, you should eat the alms of the country.
dhp309:1: Four things befall the heedless man
dhp309:2: who lies down with the wife of another:
dhp309:3:
a wealth of demerit;
a lack of good sleep;
dhp310:1: A wealth of demerit, an evil destination,
dhp310:2: & the brief delight of a fearful man with a fearful woman,
dhp310:3: & the king inflicts a harsh punishment.
dhp310:4: So no man should lie down with the wife of another.
dhp311:1: Just as sharp-bladed grass, if wrongly held,
dhp311:2: wounds the very hand that holds it–
dhp311:3: the contemplative life, if wrongly grasped,
dhp313:4: kicks up all the more dust.
dhp314:1: It’s better to leave a misdeed undone.
dhp314:3: Better that a good deed be done
dhp314:4: that, after you’ve done it, won’t make you burn.
dhp315:4: Don’t let the moment pass by.
dhp315:5: Those for whom the moment is past
dhp317:1: Seeing danger where there is none,
dhp318:1: Imagining error where there is none,
dhp318:2: and no error where there is,
